cycle.note: Locked: the Dec 1974 low is essentially undisputed historical fact (see module docstring for independent confirming sources), not subject to promotion. Unlike ^GSPC's GSC (III), no (III)-equivalent peak label is offered here -- Norris's cited ~1966 start predates DJI.json's own 1971-01-04 data range, so there is nothing in this repo's own fetched data to verify it against.

labels.granularity_note: value is a CLOSE, not a true intraday extreme -- this source only carries real intraday OHLC from 1978-01-03 onward; earlier rows repeat the close into o/h/l
value is a CLOSE, not a true intraday extreme -- this source only carries real intraday OHLC from 1978-01-03 onward; earlier rows repeat the close into o/h/l
labels.note: start of (V) = the (IV) trough, same locked convention as ^GSPC's GSC (V) (dashboard_build_spec.md §9), one degree lower

primary_label.description: Project-derived hypothesis: a single Primary-degree advance in progress since the 1974 Cycle (IV) trough, per the Cycle (V) framing above. NOT subdivided into Primary I-V internal waves here -- same reasoning as ^GSPC's Supercycle primary_label: the only pivot detector available ('slow' scale, Intermediate/40wk-18mo degree) does not resolve multi-decade structure distinctly from intermediate-scale structure, so asserting specific Primary I-V turn dates from it would overstate precision. Left for human review on the deep-history panel.

alternates.label: Sid Norris, ElliottWavePredictions.com / ElliottWavePlus.com: the DJIA's 1966-1974 decline/consolidation is a Cycle-degree fourth-wave EXPANDING TRIANGLE (wave E = 1973-1974, DJIA down 46.59% peak to trough, triangle spanning eight years); Cycle wave (V) began with the 1975-1976 breadth surge
alternates.caveat: An independent trader's own published count, not an institutional consensus -- treated as a candidate hypothesis per §3, not ground truth. Also states a SEPARATE, later Supercycle-degree count (a matching expanding triangle 'one degree larger,' active since 2000) that is out of scope here -- Cycle degree is the only claim reused.

alternates.label: General EW-practitioner consensus (as summarized across multiple search results, not independently verified against one primary text): Grand Supercycle from 1932, with 'much disparity since 1982' on subsequent sub-wave counts
alternates.caveat: Out-of-scope background only -- Grand Supercycle/Supercycle degree is ^GSPC's bootstrap's claim (dashboard_build_spec.md §9), not re-litigated here. Included only because it independently confirms the 1973-1974 crash's severity and the Dec 6, 1974 date/577.60 level used as this bootstrap's own fixed anchor.

invalidation.basis: A confirmed daily close below the real 1974 low would invalidate the Cycle (V)/Primary-in-progress hypothesis.

note: DIA has no independent bootstrap; it mechanically inherits ^DJI's cycle-degree anchor (1974) per dashboard_build_spec.md §2/§8, regenerated every build run and never hand-edited. That anchor predates DIA's own history (starts 1998-01-20), so there is nothing to substitute this phase -- own_anchor_substitutions is empty by construction, not omitted.
